security_deposit.allowed_deductions_rule: - Permissible deductions: Unpaid rent, damage beyond normal wear and tear, other amounts owed under the lease.
security_deposit.penalty_for_bad_faith_rule: - Penalty: If landlord fails to return deposit or provide itemized deductions within 30 days, landlord may be liable for the entire deposit plus attorney's fees.
security_deposit.deposit_storage_segregation_rule: - Separate account: Not specifically required by Louisiana statute.
security_deposit.special_rules_local_overrides_note: Local ordinances and special housing programs can add stricter rules.

eviction.nonpayment_notice_type: - Nonpayment of rent: Louisiana law does not require pre-litigation notice for nonpayment; landlord typically files directly for eviction. Some leases provide for a 5-day grace period.
eviction.lease_violation_notice_type: Varies — confirm notice type and cure rules.
eviction.holdover_notice_type: - Month-to-month termination: 10 days written notice required (La. Civ. Code art. 2728). This is shorter than most states.
eviction.court_process_summary_rule: - Court process: Landlord files 'rule for possession' in justice of the peace court or city court; expedited process for nonpayment.

landlord_entry.allowed_entry_reasons_rule: - Entry for repairs: Landlord may enter to make repairs or provide services. - Entry to show: Landlord may enter to show premises to prospective tenants or buyers.
landlord_entry.entry_time_window_rule: - Entry times: Entry should be at reasonable times.
landlord_entry.penalty_for_illegal_entry_rule: Varies — confirm remedies (injunction, damages, attorney fees) under state law.

rent_increase.fixed_term_increase_allowed_rule: - Fixed-term leases: Rent cannot be increased during the lease term.
rent_increase.month_to_month_increase_allowed_rule: - Rent increases: No statutory limit on amount or frequency for month-to-month tenancies.
rent_increase.delivery_method_rule: - Notice required: For month-to-month tenancies, at least 10 days advance written notice before the rent due date.
rent_increase.retaliation_protection_note: Retaliation protections may limit rent increases after protected tenant complaints; confirm local/state rules.

habitability.landlord_repair_duty_rule: - Landlord's duty: Deliver the premises in good condition; make repairs during the lease; maintain premises suitable for its intended purpose.
habitability.tenant_termination_for_unrepaired_condit: 3. Termination: Tenant may terminate the lease if premises become unusable for their intended purpose.
habitability.code_enforcement_contact_rule: Varies — contact local housing/code enforcement (city/county) for inspections and official repair orders.
